brief: Broad foundational certification covering active defense, network security, cryptography, and incident handling. Ideal for security professionals moving from general IT into a dedicated security role.
Entry-level GIAC credential covering core security concepts, networking basics, and foundational security practices. Suited for non-technical professionals entering the information security field.
Validates skills in network and endpoint defense, malware detection, and security operations. Targets security practitioners responsible for defending enterprise environments.
Covers perimeter defense, firewall architecture, packet filtering, and network access control. Suited for network security engineers and firewall administrators.
Validates continuous security monitoring skills including SIEM, log analysis, network traffic analysis, and security tool operations. Designed for SOC analysts and security engineers.
Focuses on incident response and defense for industrial control systems (ICS) and OT environments. Targets security professionals protecting critical infrastructure.
Validates penetration testing skills including reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, and reporting. Covers the full pentest methodology and is widely recognized in the industry.
Demonstrates skills in web application security testing including OWASP Top 10 exploitation, injection attacks, and authentication flaws. Suited for web application security testers.
Validates skills in network traffic analysis, protocol analysis, and forensic investigation of network-based attacks. Targets forensic analysts and network security investigators.
Covers security assessment of mobile applications and devices on iOS and Android platforms. Designed for penetration testers and security analysts working in mobile environments.
Validates skills in gathering, analyzing, and applying threat intelligence to defend organizations. Targets threat intelligence analysts and senior SOC professionals.
Covers open-source intelligence gathering techniques used in reconnaissance and investigations. Suited for penetration testers, investigators, and threat intelligence analysts.
Advanced credential focused on detecting and responding to sophisticated nation-state-level threat actors. Targets senior blue team practitioners and security engineers at mature organizations.

full_name: GIAC Exploit Researcher and Advanced Penetration Tester
brief: Elite certification covering advanced exploitation, shellcode development, and vulnerability research. One of the most technically demanding GIAC credentials, targeting experienced exploit developers and advanced penetration testers.

full_name: GIAC Assessing and Auditing Wireless Networks
brief: Validates wireless network security assessment skills including 802.11 attacks, WPA/WPA2 cracking, and rogue access point detection. Designed for penetration testers assessing wireless environments.

full_name: GIAC Strategic Planning, Policy, and Leadership
brief: Covers security strategy, policy development, and leadership for senior security practitioners. Targets CISOs and security directors responsible for organizational security programs.
Advanced forensic analysis of mobile devices including acquisition, analysis, and reporting on iOS and Android artifacts. Targets digital forensic investigators specializing in mobile evidence.
Validates skills in detecting, responding to, and recovering from security incidents. Covers attack techniques, containment strategies, and incident response methodology.
Covers Windows forensic examination including file system analysis, registry forensics, browser artifacts, and evidence collection. Targets forensic examiners and incident responders.
Advanced forensic analysis covering memory forensics, timeline analysis, and investigation of sophisticated attacks. Suited for experienced forensic analysts and DFIR professionals.

Validates skills in static and dynamic malware analysis, reverse engineering, and understanding malicious code behavior. Targets malware analysts, threat researchers, and advanced incident responders.

24-hour hands-on lab exam requiring candidates to compromise multiple machines and submit a professional pentest report. Widely considered the gold standard practical credential for penetration testers.

Advanced penetration testing credential focused on evading modern defenses, bypassing AV/EDR, and attacking hardened enterprise environments. Targets experienced pentesters looking to level up their offensive toolkit.
Hands-on credential covering Windows exploit development including buffer overflows, SEH exploitation, and ROP chain construction. Targets security researchers and exploit developers.

Advanced web application security credential focusing on source code review, white-box testing, and chaining vulnerabilities for full application compromise. Targets experienced web application penetration testers.

Elite exploit development credential covering advanced Windows exploitation, kernel bugs, and modern mitigation bypass techniques. Considered one of the most technically demanding certifications in the offensive security field.
Covers defensive security analysis from an attacker's perspective, including log analysis, SIEM usage, and detection of offensive techniques. Bridges the gap between red and blue team thinking.

Hands-on wireless security certification covering 802.11 attacks, WEP/WPA cracking, and rogue access point attacks. Practical exam requiring candidates to compromise wireless networks in a live environment.

Combined elite credential awarded upon passing OSEP, OSED, and OSWE — demonstrating mastery across advanced pentesting, exploit development, and web application exploitation. One of the rarest credentials in offensive security.

Covers incident response methodology, threat hunting, and forensic analysis from an offensive security perspective. Targets analysts who want to understand attacker techniques to improve their response capabilities.

Broad ethical hacking certification covering attack phases, common tools, and vulnerability exploitation techniques. Widely listed on government and enterprise job postings, though criticized by practitioners for its multiple-choice exam format.
Hands-on companion to the CEH, requiring candidates to perform real attacks in a live lab environment. Adds practical credibility to the standard CEH credential.
Combined designation awarded upon passing both the CEH knowledge exam and the CEH Practical. Demonstrates both theoretical understanding and hands-on ethical hacking capability.
Covers digital forensics methodology, evidence acquisition, file system analysis, and investigation of cybercrime. Targets forensic investigators and law enforcement personnel working cyber cases.
Validates incident handling and response skills including preparation, detection, containment, and recovery. Suited for security analysts and incident response team members.

Covers advanced penetration testing methodology including pen testing frameworks, documentation, and reporting. Positioned as the next step after CEH for security analysts focused on structured assessments.

Advanced practical penetration testing credential requiring candidates to conduct a full pentest engagement against a complex multi-layered network. Targets senior penetration testers seeking EC-Council's highest offensive designation.
Hands-on penetration testing credential covering network, web, IoT, and OT environments in a live range. Passing with high marks simultaneously awards the LPT Master designation.

Covers network defense strategies, threat detection, and security operations from a blue team perspective. Designed for network administrators and security engineers responsible for protecting network infrastructure.

Covers cryptographic algorithms, PKI, encryption standards, and their practical security implications. Suited for security professionals who need a solid cryptography foundation in their assessment or engineering work.

Five-day hands-on practical exam simulating a real-world network penetration test including Active Directory attacks and a professional report submission. A highly respected practitioner-focused alternative to CEH for those on a budget.
Entry-level hands-on penetration testing credential covering internal network assessment fundamentals and basic Active Directory attacks. Designed for beginners transitioning into offensive security.

Introductory hands-on credential covering malware analysis, static and dynamic analysis techniques, and basic reverse engineering. Suited for entry-level analysts wanting to break into malware research.
Practical mobile application security testing credential covering iOS and Android app assessment methodology. Targets penetration testers expanding into mobile application security.
Vendor-neutral penetration testing certification covering planning, scoping, information gathering, attacks, and reporting. Meets DoD 8570 requirements and targets mid-level penetration testers.
Advanced practitioner-level credential covering enterprise security architecture, risk management, and technical integration of security controls. Targets senior security engineers and architects.

Industry-standard entry-level security certification covering foundational security concepts, threats, vulnerabilities, and security controls. Required by DoD 8570 and widely used as a baseline credential for security roles.

Covers threat detection, behavioral analytics, and security operations center (SOC) practices. Targets security analysts responsible for monitoring and responding to threats in enterprise environments.

Entry-level hands-on penetration testing certification covering basic network attacks, web application testing, and host-based exploitation. Widely recommended as a first practical credential before pursuing OSCP.
Intermediate practical penetration testing credential covering network pentesting, web application attacks, Active Directory, and report writing. Often used as a stepping stone between eJPT and OSCP.

Hands-on web application penetration testing certification covering OWASP vulnerabilities, injection attacks, and authentication bypass. Targets penetration testers specializing in web application security.

Advanced penetration testing credential covering red team operations, advanced Active Directory attacks, and evasion techniques. Targets experienced penetration testers ready to advance to red team engagements.
Practical mobile application penetration testing credential covering Android and iOS app security assessment. Targets penetration testers working in mobile security assessment roles.

Covers network defense from a penetration tester's perspective, bridging offensive and defensive security skills. Suited for analysts who want to strengthen detection capabilities by understanding attacker methods.

UK-based penetration testing credential validating foundational penetration testing knowledge across infrastructure and web application testing. Required by many UK government and financial sector contracts.

full_name: CREST Certified Infrastructure Tester / Certified Web Application Tester
brief: Senior-level CREST penetration testing qualification covering advanced infrastructure or web application testing techniques. Required for high-assurance UK government and financial sector penetration testing engagements.
Validates management and planning skills for red team and CBEST/TIBER-style simulated attack engagements. Targets senior red team managers leading advanced threat simulation programs.
Covers host-based forensic analysis and intrusion investigation including memory analysis and artifact examination. Targets incident response analysts working host-based investigations.
Validates advanced threat intelligence management skills for leading intelligence programs at strategic, operational, and tactical levels. Targets senior threat intelligence professionals.

Covers application security assessment skills including code review, dynamic testing, and vulnerability identification in software. Suited for application security testers and secure development professionals.

Validates proficiency with the EnCase digital forensics platform for evidence acquisition, analysis, and reporting. Recognized by law enforcement and corporate investigators working with EnCase toolsets.
Validates proficiency with the FTK (Forensic Toolkit) digital forensics platform for evidence processing and analysis. Recognized in corporate and law enforcement digital forensics roles.
Validates skills in mobile device evidence extraction and physical analysis using Cellebrite tools. Widely used by law enforcement and corporate investigators for mobile forensic casework.

Advanced Cellebrite credential covering comprehensive digital intelligence workflows, advanced extraction methods, and artifact interpretation. Targets senior digital forensic examiners and investigators.

Vendor-specific forensic credential validating use of McAfee/Trellix endpoint forensic tools for investigation and incident response. Suited for security analysts operating in McAfee-centric environments.

Hands-on defensive security certification covering phishing analysis, threat intelligence, SIEM investigation, and digital forensics basics. Designed as an entry point for aspiring SOC analysts and blue team practitioners.

Advanced hands-on blue team credential covering threat hunting, advanced SIEM analysis, malware analysis, and incident response. Targets experienced SOC analysts and blue teamers looking to advance their skills.

Covers forensic investigation and incident response within HIPAA-regulated healthcare environments. Targets healthcare security professionals responsible for breach investigation and regulatory compliance.

Validates skills in threat investigation and response using Microsoft Sentinel, Microsoft Defender, and related Microsoft security tools. Targets SOC analysts operating in Microsoft-centric enterprise environments.

Entry-level Splunk credential covering basic data search, reporting, and dashboard creation within the Splunk platform. Suitable for analysts beginning to work with Splunk for log analysis and security monitoring.

Covers advanced Splunk searches, field extractions, transforming commands, and creating complex visualizations. Targets analysts who need deeper Splunk capabilities for security investigations and SOC operations.

Validates Splunk administration skills including configuration, data onboarding, user management, and platform maintenance. Targets security engineers responsible for maintaining Splunk infrastructure in a SOC.

Advanced Splunk credential covering the Splunk Enterprise Security (ES) SIEM, including correlation searches, threat intelligence integration, and incident review workflows. Targets SIEM engineers and senior SOC analysts.

Hands-on web application security certification requiring candidates to exploit real vulnerabilities using Burp Suite in a time-limited exam. Recognized by web application security professionals and bug bounty hunters.

Validates Python scripting skills applied to security testing, including writing exploit scripts, automating reconnaissance, and building security tooling. Targets penetration testers who want to extend their scripting capabilities.

full_name: eLearnSecurity Web Application Penetration Tester eXtreme
brief: Advanced web application penetration testing credential covering complex vulnerability chaining, advanced injection attacks, and sophisticated evasion techniques. Targets experienced web application security testers.

Covers cloud security assessment fundamentals including misconfigurations, identity and access management risks, and cloud-native attack paths. Suited for penetration testers and security analysts working in cloud environments.

Validates skills in assessing and securing AWS, Azure, and GCP environments including cloud-specific attack techniques and defense strategies. Targets cloud security engineers and penetration testers.

Covers IT audit, control, and assurance including security assessment frameworks and risk-based auditing. Widely recognized in audit, compliance, and governance roles assessing organizational security posture.

Covers vulnerability management program design, assessment methodologies, and remediation prioritization at enterprise scale. Targets vulnerability management engineers and security analysts responsible for continuous assessment.
Advanced red team operations credential covering adversary simulation, command and control, and advanced evasion techniques aligned with MITRE ATT&CK. Targets experienced red team operators.

full_name: GIAC Defending Advanced Threats and Forensics Incident Response
brief: Combines advanced threat defense and forensic incident response skills for seasoned DFIR practitioners. Targets senior incident handlers and forensic analysts responding to sophisticated intrusions.
Government-approved foundational security certification required for many DoD and federal contractor IA roles. Covers threats, vulnerabilities, architecture, and security operations fundamentals.

full_name: Advanced Incident Response, Threat Hunting, and Digital Forensics (FOR508 course cert)
brief: Advanced incident response and threat hunting training credential covering memory forensics, timeline analysis, and hunting for sophisticated attackers across enterprise environments.

full_name: Hacker Tools, Techniques, and Incident Handling (SEC504 course cert)
brief: Covers attacker techniques and the incident handling process, teaching defenders to think offensively. Foundational for security analysts and incident responders wanting to understand and counter real attack methods.
Validates network intrusion analysis skills including traffic analysis, pattern recognition, and investigation of network-based attacks. Targets SOC analysts and network security investigators.
Hands-on incident response credential covering detection, containment, eradication, and recovery processes. Targets security analysts and incident response team members building practical IR skills.
Practical digital forensics credential covering evidence acquisition, file system analysis, memory forensics, and forensic reporting. Targets aspiring digital forensic examiners and DFIR analysts.
